issue.title: [Issue]: Bare-string context injections are silently dropped by the engine normalizer (legacy-format migration lost)

coreClaim: Legacy bare-string context injections are preserved as prose-guardian injections in engine generation normalization.

preflight.evidence: gh PR search for issue 2170 returned no matches; keyword search found only older adjacent agent PRs; local branch/worktree search found no 2170 branch; issue assigned to @me.

dependencies.evidence: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile completed in the issue worktree and did not change dependency declarations.

reproduction.method: Vitest regression test for regenerate merge over legacy string contextInjections.
evidence: scratch/issue-2170-before.json: failing test shows only the new secret-plot row remains and the legacy prose-guardian row is missing.

verification.baselineEvidence: Passed after resolving upstream/refactor conflicts: line endings, architecture, frontend/typecheck, Rust cargo check, docs, discovery metadata, launcher safety, and unused-code checks.
focusedProof: Before fix, the focused Vitest test failed because the regenerate patch dropped the legacy prose-guardian row.
After fix, the focused Vitest test passed and preserved legacy string plus current object rows while appending the new override.
After review follow-up, the focused Vitest suite passed with a direct target lookup fallback row.
After upstream/refactor conflict resolution, start-generation.test.ts passed with issue-2170 tests and upstream agent-injection-review tests combined.
edgeCases: Existing object-shaped injection remains preserved while merging a new override.
Blank legacy string entries are skipped instead of becoming empty prose-guardian injections.
Regenerate merge falls back to direct target lookup when the loaded history list does not contain the target message extra.
verification.visualProof: Non-UI logic proof captured as raw Vitest JSON output in scratch/issue-2170-before.json, scratch/issue-2170-after.json, scratch/issue-2170-after-review.json, and scratch/issue-2170-after-conflict-resolution.json.

riskClaimMatrix.coreClaim: Legacy bare-string context injections are preserved as prose-guardian injections in engine generation normalization.
entrypoints: startGeneration regenerate path merging existing message extra.contextInjections
legacyPathsOrFormats: bare string entries meaning prose-guardian text
positiveRowsTested: legacy bare string plus new object override merge
existing object-shaped injection plus new object override merge
legacy bare string merge when the regenerated target extra is recovered by direct lookup fallback
negativeRowsTested: blank legacy string entry is skipped

groundTruthFacts.fact: Legacy bare string context injection entries map to prose-guardian injections.
groundTruthFacts.evidence: Issue #2170 regression notes cite main packages/server/src/routes/generate/agent-normalizers.ts mapping string entries to prose-guardian objects.

groundTruthFacts.fact: The refactor UI display normalizer still treats string entries as prose-guardian injections.
groundTruthFacts.evidence: src/features/modes/roleplay/components/ContextInjectionPanel.tsx normalizes string entries to { agentType: 'prose-guardian', text }.

notes: UI/browser proof is not applicable because the bug is pure engine normalization logic.
Resolved upstream/refactor merge conflict by keeping issue-2170 verification in the singleton tracked ledger; upstream issue-2184 proof belongs to PR #2247.
Conflict-resolution focused checks passed: start-generation.test.ts and active-lorebook-scanner.test.ts.

finalDoneGate.notes: Focused repro passed after the fix, review follow-up, and upstream/refactor conflict resolution. Proof gate and pnpm check passed locally after conflict resolution. PR acceptance remains pending push, CI, Bunny, and CodeRabbit.

bunny.evidence: Conflict resolution kept issue-2170 engine/test proof plus upstream refactor changes, git diff --check passed, focused start-generation and active-lorebook scanner tests passed, proof gate passed, and pnpm check passed locally.
